scope: Every public error union alternative, diagnostic-detail union alternative, error/detail enum member, Parse_Syntax category, and Parse_Definition_Form in errors.odin; the public Codec_Callback_Error in codecs.odin; and every temporal.Error alternative in vendor/temporal/types.odin. Covered rows name public package-seam tests; inapplicable rows give construction proofs.

entries.justification: This transient provenance labels the newly selected AoT element node. Public definition conflicts are reported against the AoT container or a descendant definition; parser error construction never installs the element marker as existing or attempted.

A public parser fixture must first materialize and UTF-8-preflight the complete input. Reaching parser size arithmetic overflow requires an input/count larger than the supported process can materialize, so no safe finite public-seam fixture exists; the same checked-size category is exercised at clone, mutation, marshal, and unparse public seams.

The supported reflected float sources are f16, f32, and f64. Every finite value widens into f64, while infinities and NaNs are valid TOML float values, so no supported source value can reach this alternative.

The only supported map keys are string and named/distinct string. Conversion to base string preserves exact bytes and is injective within one Odin map, and map-key codecs are forbidden, so two distinct accepted keys cannot collide.

Valid Reference Odin RTTI already has representable type sizes, and a parsed array/map count large enough to overflow destination size arithmetic cannot be materialized as complete public TOML input on a supported process. Unmarshal has no semantic-Document overload or approved private size seam.

Unmarshal passes the same max_depth to strict parsing before typed preflight. Any semantic source path beyond the limit therefore returns wrapped Parse_Limit_Error.Maximum_Depth_Exceeded first; destination wrappers do not add semantic path depth.

contracts.evidence: scripts/check_public_api.py statically requires @(require_results) on every frozen fallible/owned/boolean public procedure and rejects declaration drift.

The parser-reachable temporal sub-errors are Invalid_Month, Invalid_Day, Invalid_Hour, Invalid_Minute, Invalid_Second, and Invalid_Offset_Minutes; the public scalar matrix asserts each exact wrapped temporal.Error and zeroes it for every non-temporal Parse_Value_Error.
